---
title: "Figure 5 (info distortion: Gaussian vs numeric Fisher) — variability comparison, macro_IR"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Compares the Information-distortion DIAGONAL computed two ways, with its 95% bootstrap CI (the
variability), to see whether the Gaussian-Fisher approximation is less variable than the numeric one:
  numeric  : Likelihood_Information_Distortion          from data/433ed13  (numeric FD Fisher)
  gaussian : Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion from data/1c2ae6f  (analytic Gaussian FIM)
N_ch auto-detected as those present in BOTH runs (the Gaussian run now covers the low N_ch 10/20/50/200
at noise 0.1, the regime where the numeric FD Fisher was expected to be unstable). Two outputs:
(1) relCI vs N_ch, the key comparison; (2) the full distortion-vs-interval CI grid.
Result: variability is EQUAL (numeric ~ Gaussian, ratio ~1) at EVERY N_ch, including N_ch=10 -- for IR
the numeric FD is already well-behaved, so the Gaussian buys speed, not lower variance.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r params}
ORD        <- c("on", "off", "unitary_current", "Num_ch_mean")
PIDX       <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB  <- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
                unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")
APPROX_COL <- c(numeric = "#D55E00", gaussian = "#0072B2")
```

```{r read}
# diagonal + bootstrap CI (0.025/0.975) of a distortion component, per (noise, N_ch, interval, param)
diag_ci <- function(df, comp) df %>%
  filter(component_path == comp, statistic == "value",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX,
         probit == "mean" | (probit == "quantile" & quantile_level %in% c(0.025, 0.975))) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         q = if_else(probit == "mean", "D", if_else(quantile_level == 0.025, "lo", "hi"))) %>%
  group_by(noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name, q) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = q, values_from = v)

read_dir <- function(pat) map_dfr(Sys.glob(pat), function(p) {
  z <- sub(".*_noise_([0-9.eE+-]+)_.*", "\\1", basename(p))
  read.csv(p, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% mutate(noise = z)
})
old <- read_dir("../data/433ed13/figure_3_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_*_battery_sim.csv")
new <- read_dir("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_*_battery_sim_G.csv")
NOISES <- as.character(sort(as.numeric(unique(new$noise))))   # whatever the Gaussian run has

num <- diag_ci(old, "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Information_Distortion")          %>% mutate(approx = "numeric")
gau <- diag_ci(new, "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion") %>% mutate(approx = "gaussian")
NCH <- sort(intersect(unique(num$Num_ch), unique(gau$Num_ch)))   # N_ch present in BOTH runs (auto; now incl. low)

dd <- bind_rows(num, gau) %>%
  filter(Num_ch %in% NCH, noise %in% NOISES,
         is.finite(D), D > 0, is.finite(lo), is.finite(hi), lo > 0) %>%
  mutate(param  = factor(param_name, levels = ORD, labels = unname(PARAM_LAB[ORD])),
         nch_f  = factor(Num_ch, levels = NCH, labels = paste0("N==", NCH)),
         noisef = factor(paste("noise", noise), levels = paste("noise", NOISES)),
         approx = factor(approx, levels = c("numeric", "gaussian")),
         relCI  = (hi - lo) / (2 * D))
```

```{r summary-by-nch, fig.width = 7.0, fig.height = 4.2}
# the key comparison now that low N_ch is available: does variability differ by N_ch? (it does not)
sm <- dd %>% group_by(approx, param, noisef, Num_ch) %>%
  summarise(relCI = median(relCI), .groups = "drop")
gs <- ggplot(sm, aes(Num_ch, relCI, colour = approx, group = approx)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .4) + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_grid(noisef ~ param, labeller = labeller(param = label_parsed)) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(10, 100, 1000, 10000), labels = c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = APPROX_COL, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression(N[ch]), y = "relative bootstrap CI of distortion diagonal",
       title = "macro_IR variability: numeric vs Gaussian Fisher — equal at every N_ch") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top",
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8), plot.title = element_text(size = 10.5, face = "bold"))
gs
ggsave("Figure_5_distortion_gauss_vs_numeric_relCI_by_Nch.pdf", gs, width = 7.0, height = 4.2)
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 8.5, fig.height = 11}
g <- ggplot(dd, aes(interval_in_tau, D, colour = approx, fill = approx, group = approx)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey70", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = .2, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = .4) + geom_point(size = .6) +
  facet_grid(param + noisef ~ nch_f, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(param = label_parsed, nch_f = label_parsed)) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(.01, .1, 1)) + scale_y_log10() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = APPROX_COL, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = APPROX_COL, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]), y = "information distortion (diagonal), 95% bootstrap CI",
       title = "macro_IR  info distortion: numeric vs Gaussian Fisher") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top",
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7.5), plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_distortion_gauss_vs_numeric_lines.pdf", g, width = 8.5, height = 11)
```

```{r table}
# median relative CI half-width (the variability we are comparing), per approximation and noise
dd %>% group_by(noise, approx) %>%
  summarise(median_relCI = median(relCI), mean_relCI = mean(relCI), n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = approx, values_from = c(median_relCI, mean_relCI, n))
```
